steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: Left of the blank: “the strings inside the” is an incomplete noun phrase. Right of the blank: “are plucked not struck” is the verb phrase. Together, they form one independent clause where the noun phrase is the subject and the verb phrase completes the thought.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Within a single clause, no punctuation separates a subject from its verb. B is correct because it keeps the subject and verb connected. A period (D) ends the sentence prematurely, a colon (A) doesn't belong within clauses, and an em dash (C) creates an unjustified break. That matches choice B — B is correct.
